title: Kureha Saves Dalton
description: On pages 3–6, this part focuses on kureha saves dalton, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: Kureha Saves Dalton. Back in the present, Wapol attacks Drum Castle and tries to turn Hiriluk’s symbol into another selfish possession. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Dalton is still alive,

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: His body and vitals are merely frozen, that's all!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: So if you don't mind,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Let us MD-20s treat him!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: I thought there weren't any other doctors on this island?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Maybe they were sailing together with Wapol on his ship?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: But then... are they bad guys too?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Hold it right there!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: You expect us to trust you people who've played yes-men to Wapol all this time!?

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Just what do you plan to do with Dalton!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: We're your only hope if you want to save him!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: It's true that we went along with Wapol's orders,

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: But that doesn't change the fact that we're still doctors at heart...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: And as doctors, all our research and efforts were dedicated to saving the sick and wounded!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Long ago,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: A certain quack doctor taught us to never give up...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: This country cannot afford to lose any more brave fools like him...!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Let me show you...

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: The true power of the Baku Baku Fruit...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: He can still stand up after taking that hit?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Guy's pretty strong, I have to admit.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: your majesty!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: chess! List my meals since this morning...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: At once, your majesty! Let's see... one butter-saltered cannon, one raw cannon, a cannonball & gunpowder salad,

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: and one grilled house from the village.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: The heck does he eat?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Calling him an ownvore would be the under- statement of the decade...

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Watch closely... the true power of my Baku Baku Fruit can be seen only after a meal...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What's he planning to do?

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: The very things I consume can become my new flesh and blood...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Baku Baku Shock!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: What the...!

summary: This part highlights kureha saves dalton and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
